dosing: Most commonly prescribed as a tapering course starting at 60mg/day, though starting doses range from 40mg to 100mg depending on severity
Used as a short-term bridge to break a cycle or buy time while slower preventives like verapamil take effect
Repeated courses become less effective over time, and many patients report the attacks return as soon as the taper ends
preparations: Oral prednisone or prednisolone tablets are the standard form
Pre-packaged steroid dose packs (Medrol) are convenient but may not provide a high enough starting dose for cluster headache
schedule: Typical taper runs 10-14 days: start at the full dose for 2-3 days, then reduce by 10mg every 2-3 days
Attacks frequently break through during the lower end of the taper, especially below 30mg
Should be used sparingly; frequent steroid courses carry serious long-term health risks
